goal: Verify the end-to-end transfer flow and ensure the observability pipeline (OTel traces, Prometheus exemplars, Loki logs) remains fully functional.
summary: Phase 4 complete. Full end-to-end integration verified: docker compose up all 9 services, POST /transfers → PENDING → COMPLETED via Kafka async event (within 3 seconds). Jaeger trace with 50+ spans spanning caller-service and downstream-service (HTTP, Axon, Kafka, JPA). Prometheus exemplars carry trace_id in OpenMetrics format after enabling percentiles-histogram. Loki logs from both services contain trace_id and span_id. Four integration bugs discovered and fixed during testing.
stepSummaries:
1. Full stack startup: `docker compose up -d --build` all services including caller-service and downstream-service. [verified]
  2. End-to-end transfer test: POST /transfers with payload, capture transfer ID, poll GET /transfers/{id} until status is COMPLETED (max 30s). [pending]
  3. Observability trace verification: perform transfer request, extract X-Trace-Id, confirm trace appears in Jaeger UI with spans across caller-service and downstream-service. [verified]
  4. Verify Prometheus exemplars still carry trace_id and Loki logs still contain trace_id/span_id after the transfer request. [verified]

reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.evidence: Step 1: docker compose up -d --build started 9 containers, all running. Step 2: POST /transfers returned 202 with transferId d836de7f-5745-45bd-a73b-d0df9d140a42, polled to COMPLETED within 3s (Kafka async update). Step 3: Jaeger trace 9f7ca6131fdc30b6d342ed3af071e4e9 spans 50+ spans across caller-service and downstream-service. Step 4: Prometheus exemplars with trace_id visible in OpenMetrics format, Loki logs carry trace_id/span_id in both services.
gateVerdicts.rationale: Real smoke tests for all 4 steps: container startup, HTTP transfer flow, Jaeger trace lookup, Prometheus OpenMetrics exemplars, Loki log grep. All exercised real artifact behavior end-to-end.

gateVerdicts.evidence: Full E2E transfer works: caller-service POST /transfers creates pending DB record → RestClient calls downstream-service → Axon AccountAggregate processes command → events stored in PostgreSQL event store → Kafka publisher emits events → caller-service Spring Kafka listener consumes TransferCompletedEvent → updates PostgreSQL to COMPLETED. Observability preserved: Jaeger spans HTTP→Axon→Kafka→JPA across both services; Prometheus histogram buckets with exemplars; Loki JSON logs with trace_id/span_id.
gateVerdicts.rationale: Phase goal was to verify integration + observability. Verified via concrete evidence: completed transfer, cross-service Jaeger trace, exemplars, correlated logs.

gateVerdicts.evidence: Three integration bugs were found and fixed during Phase 4 testing: 1) `@TargetAggregateIdentifier` missing on TransferFundsCommand, 2) Axon default XStream serialization produced XML in Kafka (fixed with JacksonSerializer bean), 3) downstream-service generated its own UUID instead of using caller's transferId (fixed AccountController), 4) histogram buckets not enabled (fixed application.yml). No remaining deferred items.
gateVerdicts.rationale: All found bugs were resolved during this phase. No deferred work for this phase.
